views.name: fuel-breakdown
views.title: Global CO₂ Emissions by Fuel Type (1900–2024)
views.description: Fossil fuel CO₂ emissions split by source: solid fuels (coal) dominated through the mid-20th century, then liquid fuels (oil) surged with post-war growth, and gas fuels rose steadily. Cement has grown sharply since 2000, driven by Chinese construction. Total emissions have risen 20× since 1900.
